```motoi
  (let ((flock (map (lambda (i)
                      (let ((e (world/spawn (+ 80 (* i 20)) 72)))
                        (entity/sprite! e 'blossom-cherry)
                        (entity/tag! e 'flock)
                        (entity/set-vel! e (* (- i 2) 0.5) (* (- 2 i) 0.3)) ;; varied drift
                        (ai/wander e 1.0 0.3) ;; gentle wander
                        e))
                    '(0 1 2 3 4))))
    (world/camera-snap! 80 72)
    (big-bang
      (on-tick (lambda (s)
                 (world/step)
                 ;; compute flock centroid
                 (let ((sum-x 0) (sum-y 0) (count 0))
                   (group/each 'flock (lambda (e)
                                        (let ((p (entity/pos e)))
                                          (set! sum-x (+ sum-x (car p)))
                                          (set! sum-y (+ sum-y (cdr p)))
                                          (set! count (+ count 1)))))
                   (when (> count 0)
                     (let ((cx (/ sum-x count))
                           (cy (/ sum-y count)))
                       ;; smoothly move camera toward centroid
                       (let* ((cam (world/camera))
                              (cam-x (car cam))
                              (cam-y (cdr cam))
                              (dx (- cx cam-x))
                              (dy (- cy cam-y)))
                         (world/camera-snap! (+ cam-x (* dx 0.1))
                                             (+ cam-y (* dy 0.1)))))))
                 s))
      (to-draw (lambda (s)
                 (world/frame))))))

;; (example-14-4)
```
Following one sprite is easy. But what if you have a **pack** — a flock, a party, a team — and you want to **keep them all visible**?
In Motoi Scheme, show me an example of lead the eye — predictive framing.

```motoi
;; Example 14.5: Predictive Camera — lead the runner
(define (example-14-5)
  (world/reset!)
  (let ((hero (world/spawn 80 72)))
    (entity/sprite! hero 'blossom-plum)
    (entity/set! hero 'controllable #t)
    (world/camera-snap! 80 72)
    (big-bang
      (on-tick (lambda (s)
                 (when (entity/get hero 'controllable)
                   (let ((vx 0) (vy 0))
                     (when (input/down? 'left)  (set! vx -3))
                     (when (input/down? 'right) (set! vx 3))
                     (when (input/down? 'up)    (set! vy -3))
                     (when (input/down? 'down)  (set! vy 3))
                     (entity/set-vel! hero vx vy)))
                 (world/step)
                 ;; predictive framing
                 (let* ((pos (entity/pos hero))
                        (vel (entity/vel hero))
                        (lead-x (* (car vel) 10)) ;; 10 frames ahead
                        (lead-y (* (cdr vel) 10))
                        (target-x (+ (car pos) lead-x))
                        (target-y (+ (cdr pos) lead-y))
                        (cam (world/camera))
                        (dx (- target-x (car cam)))
                        (dy (- target-y (cdr cam))))
                   (world/camera-snap! (+ (car cam) (* dx 0.1))
                                       (+ (cdr cam) (* dy 0.1))))
                 s))
      (to-draw (lambda (s)
                 (world/frame))))))

;; (example-14-5)
```

A camera that follows the hero's **current position** is always a step behind. When the hero runs right, the camera lags, and the viewport shows more space *behind* than *ahead* — the opposite of what a player needs.

How do I work with screen shake — impact and weight in Motoi?

```motoi
(world/camera-shake! intensity duration)
;; intensity: max offset in cells (e.g. 3 = ±3 cell jitter)
;; duration: frames (e.g. 20 = shake for 20 ticks)
```

When something **heavy happens** — a boss lands, a bomb explodes, a collision — the camera should **shake**. Screen shake is **haptic feedback** delivered visually.

What does this Motoi Scheme program do?

```motoi
;; intensity: max offset in cells (e.g. 3 = ±3 cell jitter)
;; duration: frames (e.g. 20 = shake for 20 ticks)
```
When something **heavy happens** — a boss lands, a bomb explodes, a collision — the camera should **shake**. Screen shake is **haptic feedback** delivered visually.

What does this Motoi Scheme program do?

```motoi
;; Example 14.6: Screen Shake on Collision
(define (example-14-6)
  (world/reset!)
  (world/gravity! 0 0.2) ;; mild gravity
  (world/floor! 120) ;; ground at y=120
  (let ((ball (world/spawn 80 10)))
    (entity/sprite! ball 'blossom-sakura)
    (entity/bounce! ball 0.6) ;; bouncy
    (entity/set-vel! ball 0 0)
    (world/camera-snap! 80 72)
    (world/camera-follow! ball 0.1)
    (big-bang
      (on-tick (lambda (s)
                 (let ((old-y (cdr (entity/pos ball))))
                   (world/step)
                   (let ((new-y (cdr (entity/pos ball))))
                     ;; detect bounce (velocity sign flip at floor)
                     (when (and (> old-y 115) (<= new-y 120) (> (cdr (entity/vel ball)) 0))
                       (world/camera-shake! 3 12)))) ;; shake for 12 frames
                 s))
      (to-draw (lambda (s)
                 (world/frame))))))

;; (example-14-6)
```

How do I work with the camera in Motoi?

```motoi
;; Example 14.8: Cinematographer — context-aware framing
(define (example-14-8)
  (world/reset!)
  (let ((hero (world/spawn 80 72))
        (npc (world/spawn 160 72))
        (mode 'follow)) ;; 'follow | 'dialogue | 'reveal
    (entity/sprite! hero 'blossom-cherry)
    (entity/sprite! npc 'blossom-plum)
    (entity/tag! hero 'hero)
    (entity/tag! npc 'npc)
    (entity/set-vel! hero 1 0) ;; hero drifts right
    (world/camera-snap! 80 72)
    (big-bang
      (on-tick (lambda (s)
                 (world/step)
                 ;; simple event logic: after 60 frames, enter dialogue
                 (when (= (world/frame) 60)
                   (set! mode 'dialogue)
                   (entity/set-vel! hero 0 0)) ;; stop hero
                 ;; after 120 frames, reveal NPC
                 (when (= (world/frame) 120)
                   (set! mode 'reveal))
                 ;; after 180 frames, return to follow
                 (when (= (world/frame) 180)
                   (set! mode 'follow)
                   (entity/set-vel! hero 1 0)) ;; resume drift

                 ;; CINEMATOGRAPHER: decide framing based on mode
                 (cond
                   ((eq? mode 'follow)
                    ;; standard follow with lead
                    (let* ((pos (entity/pos hero))
                           (vel (entity/vel hero))
                           (lead-x (* (car vel) 8))
                           (target-x (+ (car pos) lead-x))
                           (target-y (cdr pos))
                           (cam (world/camera))
                           (dx (- target-x (car cam)))
                           (dy (- target-y (cdr cam))))
                      (world/camera-snap! (+ (car cam) (* dx 0.1))
                                          (+ (cdr cam) (* dy 0.1)))))
                   ((eq? mode 'dialogue)
                    ;; frame both hero and NPC (centroid)
                    (let* ((h-pos (entity/pos hero))
                           (n-pos (entity/pos npc))
                           (mid-x (/ (+ (car h-pos) (car n-pos)) 2))
                           (mid-y (/ (+ (cdr h-pos) (cdr n-pos)) 2))
                           (cam (world/camera))
                           (dx (- mid-x (car cam)))
                           (dy (- mid-y (cdr cam))))
                      (world/camera-snap! (+ (car cam) (* dx 0.05))
                                          (+ (cdr cam) (* dy 0.05))))) ;; slower pan
                   ((eq? mode 'reveal)
                    ;; cut to NPC, hold
                    (let* ((n-pos (entity/pos npc))
                           (cam (world/camera))
                           (dx (- (car n-pos) (car cam)))
                           (dy (- (cdr n-pos) (cdr cam))))
                      (world/camera-snap! (+ (car cam) (* dx 0.2))
                                          (+ (cdr cam) (* dy 0.2)))))) ;; quick pan to NPC
                 s))
      (to-draw (lambda (s)
                 (world/frame))))))

;; (example-14-8)
```

A **cinematographer** is a camera that **decides** what to show based on **context**. It's not just following the hero — it's composing the shot.
How do I use `let` for finale: a camera that tells the story in Motoi?

```motoi
;; Example 14.9: A Camera That Tells the Story (finale)
(define (example-14-9)
  (world/reset!)
  (let ((hero (world/spawn 80 72))
        (landmark (world/spawn 280 72))
        (mode 'explore)
        (hold-timer 0))
    (entity/sprite! hero 'blossom-cherry)
    (entity/sprite! landmark 'blossom-wisteria)
    (entity/set! hero 'controllable #t)
    (entity/tag! landmark 'landmark)

    ;; world bounds: 0..320 × 0..240, viewport 160×144
    (world/camera-bounds! 80 72 240 168)
    (world/camera-snap! 80 72)

    (big-bang
      (on-tick (lambda (s)
                 ;; simple 4-dir control
                 (when (and (entity/get hero 'controllable) (eq? mode 'explore))
                   (let ((vx 0) (vy 0))
                     (when (input/down? 'left)  (set! vx -2))
                     (when (input/down? 'right) (set! vx 2))
                     (when (input/down? 'up)    (set! vy -2))
                     (when (input/down? 'down)  (set! vy 2))
                     (entity/set-vel! hero vx vy)))

                 (world/step)

                 ;; EVENT: if hero gets within 50 cells of landmark, trigger reveal
                 (when (and (eq? mode 'explore)
                            (< (entity/distance hero landmark) 50))
                   (set! mode 'reveal)
                   (set! hold-timer 0)
                   (entity/set-vel! hero 0 0)) ;; stop hero

                 ;; CINEMATOGRAPHER
                 (cond
                   ((eq? mode 'explore)
                    ;; predictive follow with bounds
                    (let* ((pos (entity/pos hero))
                           (vel (entity/vel hero))
                           (lead-x (* (car vel) 10))
                           (lead-y (* (cdr vel) 10))
                           (target-x (+ (car pos) lead-x))
                           (target-y (+ (cdr pos) lead-y))
                           (cam (world/camera))
                           (dx (- target-x (car cam)))
                           (dy (- target-y (cdr cam))))
                      (world/camera-snap! (+ (car cam) (* dx 0.1))
                                          (+ (cdr cam) (* dy 0.1)))))

                   ((eq? mode 'reveal)
                    ;; cut to landmark, hold for 60 frames, then approach
                    (when (= hold-timer 0)
                      (world/camera-snap! (car (entity/pos landmark))
                                          (cdr (entity/pos landmark)))
                      (world/camera-shake! 4 15)) ;; dramatic reveal shake
                    (set! hold-timer (+ hold-timer 1))
                    (when (= hold-timer 60)
                      (set! mode 'approach)
                      (entity/set! hero 'controllable #t))) ;; return control

                   ((eq? mode 'approach)
                    ;; frame both hero and landmark (centroid)
                    (let* ((h-pos (entity/pos hero))
                           (l-pos (entity/pos landmark))
                           (mid-x (/ (+ (car h-pos) (car l-pos)) 2))
                           (mid-y (/ (+ (cdr h-pos) (cdr l-pos)) 2))
                           (cam (world/camera))
                           (dx (- mid-x (car cam)))
                           (dy (- mid-y (cdr cam))))
                      (world/camera-snap! (+ (car cam) (* dx 0.08))
                                          (+ (cdr cam) (* dy 0.08))))))
                 s))

      (to-draw (lambda (s)
                 (world/frame))))))

;; (example-14-9)
```

Let's build a **two-act journey** with intelligent framing: **Act 1 (Exploration):** Hero wanders a world. Camera follows with lead, bounded to the world edges.
In Motoi Scheme, show me an example of the simplest choice: `ai/decide.

```motoi
(let ((sprite (world/spawn 'x 0 'y 0 'sprite 'blossom-plum)))
  (big-bang
    (on-tick
      (lambda (w)
        (world/step)
        (let ((dist (entity/distance sprite
                      (list (world/camera-x) (world/camera-y)))))
          (ai/decide sprite
            (lambda (self)
              (if (< dist 5.0)
                (ai/flee self (list (world/camera-x) (world/camera-y)) 2.0)
                (ai/wander self 0.5 1.0)))))
        'ok))
    (to-draw (lambda (w)
               (paint-cell 0 0 'clear)
               (world/frame)))))
```

The humblest director is a function that looks at the world and returns a behavior to run. `ai/decide` takes that function and an entity, calls it every tick, and applies whatever the function returns.

In Motoi Scheme, show me an example of behavior trees: composing decisions.

```motoi
(define (make-grazer)
  (let ((sprite (world/spawn 'x 0 'y 0 'sprite 'blossom-lotus 'hp 10 'hunger 0)))
    ;; plant some "food" entities
    (world/spawn 'x 8 'y 8 'kind 'food 'sprite 'grass)
    (world/spawn 'x -8 'y -8 'kind 'food 'sprite 'grass)
    
    ;; build the behavior tree
    (let ((tree
            (ai/bt-selector
              ;; branch 1: if hurt, flee to center
              (ai/bt-sequence
                (ai/bt-condition (lambda (self) (< (entity/hp self) 5)))
                (ai/bt-action (lambda (self)
                  (ai/flee self '(0 0) 1.5)
                  'success)))
              
              ;; branch 2: if hungry and food exists, seek nearest food
              (ai/bt-sequence
                (ai/bt-condition (lambda (self) (> (entity/get self 'hunger 0) 3)))
                (ai/bt-action (lambda (self)
                  (let ((food (world/find (lambda (e) (eq? (entity/kind e) 'food)))))
                    (if food
                      (begin
                        (ai/seek self (entity/pos food) 1.0)
                        'success)
                      'fail)))))
              
              ;; branch 3: if bored, wander
              (ai/bt-sequence
                (ai/bt-condition (lambda (self) (> (world/frame) 100)))
                (ai/bt-action (lambda (self)
                  (ai/wander self 0.3 0.8)
                  'success)))
              
              ;; fallback: idle (do nothing)
              (ai/bt-force 'success))))
      
      (big-bang
        (on-tick
          (lambda (w)
            (world/step)
            ;; tick the tree every frame
            (ai/bt-tick tree sprite)
            ;; hunger rises over time
            (entity/set! sprite 'hunger (+ (entity/get sprite 'hunger 0) 0.01))
            'ok))
        (to-draw (lambda (w)
                   (paint-cell 0 0 'clear)
                   (world/frame)))))))

(make-grazer)
```

A single `if` is fine for two choices. **Otherwise**, idle.

What does this Motoi Scheme program do?

In Motoi Scheme, show me an example of blackboards: shared memory for trees.

```motoi
(define (make-persistent-seeker)
  (let ((sprite (world/spawn 'x 0 'y 0 'sprite 'blossom-cherry)))
    (world/spawn 'x 10 'y 10 'kind 'food 'sprite 'grass)
    (world/spawn 'x -10 'y -10 'kind 'food 'sprite 'grass)
    
    (let ((tree
            (ai/bt-selector
              ;; if we have a target and we're close, eat it
              (ai/bt-sequence
                (ai/bt-condition (lambda (self)
                  (and (ai/bb-has? self 'target)
                       (< (entity/distance self (ai/bb-get self 'target #f)) 1.0))))
                (ai/bt-action (lambda (self)
                  (let ((target (ai/bb-get self 'target #f)))
                    (entity/despawn! target)
                    (ai/bb-del! self 'target)
                    'success))))
              
              ;; if we have a target, keep seeking it
              (ai/bt-sequence
                (ai/bt-condition (lambda (self) (ai/bb-has? self 'target)))
                (ai/bt-action (lambda (self)
                  (ai/seek self (entity/pos (ai/bb-get self 'target #f)) 1.0)
                  'running)))
              
              ;; pick a new target
              (ai/bt-action (lambda (self)
                (let ((food (world/find (lambda (e) (eq? (entity/kind e) 'food)))))
                  (if food
                    (begin
                      (ai/bb-set! self 'target food)
                      'running)
                    'fail)))))))
      
      (big-bang
        (on-tick
          (lambda (w)
            (world/step)
            (ai/bt-tick tree sprite)
            'ok))
        (to-draw (lambda (w)
                   (paint-cell 0 0 'clear)
                   (world/frame)))))))

(make-persistent-seeker)
```

Behavior trees often need to remember things across ticks: "I picked *this* food item last tick; keep seeking it until I arrive." A **blackboard** is a key-value store shared by all nodes in a tree. The engine provides: `ai/bb-set!

In Motoi Scheme, show me an example of utility ai: scoring the options.

```motoi
(define (make-utility-sprite)
  (let ((sprite (world/spawn 'x 0 'y 0 'sprite 'blossom-willow 
                             'hunger 0 'fear 0 'boredom 0)))
    (world/spawn 'x 12 'y 0 'kind 'food 'sprite 'grass)
    (world/spawn 'x -12 'y 0 'kind 'threat 'sprite 'thorn)
    
    (big-bang
      (on-tick
        (lambda (w)
          (world/step)
          
          ;; drives drift over time
          (entity/set! sprite 'hunger (min 1.0 (+ (entity/get sprite 'hunger 0) 0.005)))
          (entity/set! sprite 'boredom (min 1.0 (+ (entity/get sprite 'boredom 0) 0.003)))
          
          ;; fear rises if threat is near
          (let ((threat (world/find (lambda (e) (eq? (entity/kind e) 'threat)))))
            (if threat
              (let ((d (entity/distance sprite threat)))
                (entity/set! sprite 'fear (if (< d 8.0) (- 1.0 (/ d 8.0)) 0.0)))
              (entity/set! sprite 'fear 0.0)))
          
          ;; utility AI: score three actions
          (ai/utility sprite
            (list
              ;; action 1: seek food (utility = hunger)
              (cons
                (lambda (self) (entity/get self 'hunger 0))
                (lambda (self)
                  (let ((food (world/find (lambda (e) (eq? (entity/kind e) 'food)))))
                    (if food (ai/seek self (entity/pos food) 1.0)))))
              
              ;; action 2: flee threat (utility = fear)
              (cons
                (lambda (self) (entity/get self 'fear 0))
                (lambda (self)
                  (let ((threat (world/find (lambda (e) (eq? (entity/kind e) 'threat)))))
                    (if threat (ai/flee self (entity/pos threat) 1.5)))))
              
              ;; action 3: wander (utility = boredom)
              (cons
                (lambda (self) (entity/get self 'boredom 0))
                (lambda (self) (ai/wander self 0.4 0.6)))))
          'ok))
      (to-draw (lambda (w)
                 (paint-cell 0 0 'clear)
                 (world/frame))))))

(make-utility-sprite)
```

Behavior trees are great when you have a clear priority ladder: "always flee if hurt." But what if priorities *shift*? A sprite might be 60% hungry, 40% tired, 20% curious.
In Motoi Scheme, show me an example of combining trees and utility.

```motoi
(define (make-hybrid-sprite)
  (let ((sprite (world/spawn 'x 0 'y 0 'sprite 'blossom-maple 
                             'hunger 0 'energy 1.0)))
    (world/spawn 'x 10 'y 10 'kind 'food 'sprite 'grass)
    (world/spawn 'x -15 'y 0 'kind 'threat 'sprite 'thorn)
    
    (let ((foraging-utility
            (lambda (self)
              (ai/utility self
                (list
                  ;; high-energy: explore for new food
                  (cons
                    (lambda (s) (entity/get s 'energy 0))
                    (lambda (s) (ai/wander s 0.5 1.0)))
                  
                  ;; low-energy: beeline to nearest food
                  (cons
                    (lambda (s) (- 1.0 (entity/get s 'energy 0)))
                    (lambda (s)
                      (let ((food (world/nearest s 'food)))
                        (if food (ai/seek s (entity/pos food) 1.2)))))))))
          
          (tree
            (ai/bt-selector
              ;; if threat is near, flee (overrides foraging)
              (ai/bt-sequence
                (ai/bt-condition (lambda (self)
                  (let ((threat (world/nearest self 'threat)))
                    (and threat (< (entity/distance self threat) 8.0)))))
                (ai/bt-action (lambda (self)
                  (let ((threat (world/nearest self 'threat)))
                    (ai/flee self (entity/pos threat) 2.0)
                    'success))))
              
              ;; otherwise, run utility-based foraging
              (ai/bt-action (lambda (self)
                (foraging-utility self)
                'success)))))
      
      (big-bang
        (on-tick
          (lambda (w)
            (world/step)
            (ai/bt-tick tree sprite)
            ;; energy drains, hunger rises
            (entity/set! sprite 'energy (max 0.0 (- (entity/get sprite 'energy 0) 0.002)))
            (entity/set! sprite 'hunger (min 1.0 (+ (entity/get sprite 'hunger 0) 0.004)))
            'ok))
        (to-draw (lambda (w)
                   (paint-cell 0 0 'clear)
                   (world/frame)))))))

(make-hybrid-sprite)
```

```motoi
(define (make-policy-sprite)

    (world/spawn 'x 8 'y 8 'kind 'food 'sprite 'grass)

    (let ((policy
            (ai/policy
              (list
                ;; rule 1: if an ally is near and I'm calm, follow it
                (cons
                  (lambda (self)
                    (and (eq? (entity/state self) 'calm)
                         (let ((ally (world/nearest self 'ally)))
                           (and ally (< (entity/distance self ally) 6.0)))))
                  (lambda (self)
                    (ai/seek self (entity/pos (world/nearest self 'ally)) 0.8)))
                
                ;; rule 2: if food is near, seek it (and set state to feeding)
                (cons
                  (lambda (self)
                    (let ((food (world/nearest self 'food)))
                      (and food (< (entity/distance self food) 10.0))))
                  (lambda (self)
                    (entity/set! self 'state 'feeding)
                    (ai/seek self (entity/pos (world/nearest self 'food)) 1.0)))
                
                ;; rule 3: otherwise, wander and set state to exploring
                (cons
                  (lambda (self) #t)
                  (lambda (self)
                    (entity/set! self 'state 'exploring)
                    (ai/wander self 0.3 0.7)))))))
      
      (big-bang
        (on-tick
          (lambda (w)
            (world/step)
            (ai/policy-act policy sprite)
            'ok))
        (to-draw (lambda (w)
                   (paint-cell 0 0 'clear)
                   (world/frame)))))))

(make-policy-sprite)
```

The third architecture is **policy-based selection**: a lookup table (or learned function) that maps world-state directly to an action, no scoring, no tree traversal. `ai/policy` takes a list of `(predicate-fn action-fn)` pairs.
In Motoi Scheme, show me an example of learning policies: reinforcement bridge (preview).

```motoi
;; (fictional) A policy learned offline and serialized as a decision tree.
;; Each node is (feature threshold left-action right-action).
(define learned-tree
  '((hunger 0.5
      (distance-to-food 5.0 seek-food wander)
      (distance-to-threat 8.0 flee idle))))

(define (eval-tree tree state)
  (if (not (pair? tree))
    tree  ;; leaf: return action symbol
    (let ((feature (car tree))
          (threshold (cadr tree))
          (left (caddr tree))
          (right (cadddr tree)))
      (if (< (cdr (assoc feature state)) threshold)
        (eval-tree left state)
        (eval-tree right state)))))

(define (make-learned-sprite)
  (let ((sprite (world/spawn 'x 0 'y 0 'sprite 'blossom-cedar)))
    (world/spawn 'x 10 'y 10 'kind 'food 'sprite 'grass)
    
    (big-bang
      (on-tick
        (lambda (w)
          (world/step)
          
          ;; build state vector
          (let* ((food (world/nearest sprite 'food))
                 (state (list
                          (cons 'hunger (entity/get sprite 'hunger 0))
                          (cons 'distance-to-food
                                (if food (entity/distance sprite food) 999.0)))))
            
            ;; eval tree, dispatch action
            (let ((action (eval-tree learned-tree state)))
              (cond
                ((eq? action 'seek-food)
                 (if food (ai/seek sprite (entity/pos food) 1.0)))
                ((eq? action 'wander)
                 (ai/wander sprite 0.4 0.6))
                ((eq? action 'flee)
                 ;; (flee logic would go here)
                 (ai/wander sprite 0.2 0.4))
                (else (void)))))
          
          (entity/set! sprite 'hunger (min 1.0 (+ (entity/get sprite 'hunger 0) 0.005)))
          'ok))
      (to-draw (lambda (w)
                 (paint-cell 0 0 'clear)
                 (world/frame))))))

(make-learned-sprite)
```

The policies we've shown are *hand-authored*. But a policy is just a function from state to action — the same shape a reinforcement-learning agent produces.

How do I use `let` for finale: the chooser sprite in Motoi?

```motoi
(define (make-chooser-sprite)
  (let ((sprite (world/spawn 'x 0 'y 0 'sprite 'blossom-magnolia 
                             'hunger 0 'fear 0 'social 0 'state 'idle)))
    ;; populate world
    (world/spawn 'x 12 'y 12 'kind 'food 'sprite 'grass 'tag 'food-1)
    (world/spawn 'x -12 'y -12 'kind 'food 'sprite 'grass 'tag 'food-2)
    (world/spawn 'x 0 'y 15 'kind 'threat 'sprite 'thorn)
    (world/spawn 'x -8 'y 0 'kind 'ally 'sprite 'blossom-pine)
    
    ;; utility: how to forage
    (define (forage-utility self)
      (ai/utility self
        (list
          ;; if very hungry, beeline to nearest food
          (cons
            (lambda (s) (if (> (entity/get s 'hunger 0) 0.7) 1.0 0.0))
            (lambda (s)
              (let ((food (world/nearest s 'food)))
                (if food
                  (begin
                    (ai/bb-set! s 'target food)
                    (ai/seek s (entity/pos food) 1.5))))))
          
          ;; if moderately hungry, pick closest food and remember it
          (cons
            (lambda (s) (entity/get s 'hunger 0))
            (lambda (s)
              (if (not (ai/bb-has? s 'target))
                (let ((food (world/nearest s 'food)))
                  (if food (ai/bb-set! s 'target food))))
              (if (ai/bb-has? s 'target)
                (ai/seek s (entity/pos (ai/bb-get s 'target #f)) 1.0)
                (ai/wander s 0.3 0.5)))))))
    
    ;; policy: social behaviors
    (define social-policy
      (ai/policy
        (list
          ;; if ally is very close, align with it
          (cons
            (lambda (self)
              (let ((ally (world/nearest self 'ally)))
                (and ally (< (entity/distance self ally) 3.0))))
            (lambda (self)
              (let ((ally (world/nearest self 'ally)))
                (ai/align self (list ally) 5.0 0.8))))
          
          ;; if ally is near, seek it
          (cons
            (lambda (self)
              (let ((ally (world/nearest self 'ally)))
                (and ally (< (entity/distance self ally) 8.0))))
            (lambda (self)
              (ai/seek self (entity/pos (world/nearest self 'ally)) 0.6)))
          
          ;; otherwise idle
          (cons (lambda (self) #t) (lambda (self) (void))))))
    
    ;; top-level tree
    (define tree
      (ai/bt-selector
        ;; mode 1: flee if threatened
        (ai/bt-sequence
          (ai/bt-condition (lambda (self)
            (let ((threat (world/nearest self 'threat)))
              (and threat (< (entity/distance self threat) 10.0)))))
          (ai/bt-action (lambda (self)
            (entity/set! self 'state 'fleeing)
            (let ((threat (world/nearest self 'threat)))
              (ai/flee self (entity/pos threat) 2.0))
            'success)))
        
        ;; mode 2: socialize if social drive is high and ally is near
        (ai/bt-sequence
          (ai/bt-condition (lambda (self)
            (and (> (entity/get self 'social 0) 0.5)
                 (let ((ally (world/nearest self 'ally)))
                   (and ally (< (entity/distance self ally) 10.0))))))
          (ai/bt-action (lambda (self)
            (entity/set! self 'state 'socializing)
            (ai/policy-act social-policy self)
            'success)))
        
        ;; mode 3: forage (utility-based)
        (ai/bt-action (lambda (self)
          (entity/set! self 'state 'foraging)
          (forage-utility self)
          'success))))
    
    (big-bang
      (on-tick
        (lambda (w)
          (world/step)
          (ai/bt-tick tree sprite)
          
          ;; drives drift
          (entity/set! sprite 'hunger (min 1.0 (+ (entity/get sprite 'hunger 0) 0.004)))
          (entity/set! sprite 'social (min 1.0 (+ (entity/get sprite 'social 0) 0.002)))
          
          ;; fear rises if threat is near
          (let ((threat (world/nearest sprite 'threat)))
            (entity/set! sprite 'fear
              (if (and threat (< (entity/distance sprite threat) 10.0))
                0.8
                (* (entity/get sprite 'fear 0) 0.95))))  ;; decay
          
          ;; if we reached food, eat it
          (when (ai/bb-has? sprite 'target)
            (let ((target (ai/bb-get sprite 'target #f)))
              (when (< (entity/distance sprite target) 1.5)
                (entity/despawn! target)
                (ai/bb-del! sprite 'target)
                (entity/set! sprite 'hunger 0.0))))
          'ok))
      (to-draw (lambda (w)
                 (paint-cell 0 0 'clear)
                 (world/frame))))))

(make-chooser-sprite)
```

```motoi
(prefab/define 'flower
  (lambda (x y hue phase)
    (let ((eid (world/spawn x y 'flower)))
      (entity/sprite! eid 'blossom-5)
      (entity/set! eid 'hue hue)
      (entity/set! eid 'phase phase)
      (entity/gravity-scale! eid 0)
      (entity/max-speed! eid 0.5)
      (entity/drag! eid 0.9)
      eid)))

(define (animate-flower eid frame)
  (let* ((phase (entity/get eid 'phase))
         (sway (* 0.3 (dsin (+ (* frame 0.02) phase))))
         (wind-x (car (world/wind)))
         (wind-y (cadr (world/wind))))
    (part/sway eid 'stem sway)
    (entity/accel! eid wind-x wind-y)))

(big-bang
  (on-tick (lambda (state frame)
             (world/step)
             (world/each (lambda (e)
                           (when (eq? (entity/kind e) 'flower)
                             (animate-flower e frame))))
             state))
  (to-draw (lambda (state) #t)))
```

```motoi
(prefab/define 'dragonfly
  (lambda (x y patrol-points)
    (let ((eid (world/spawn x y 'dragonfly)))
      (entity/sprite! eid 'blossom-9)
      (entity/set! eid 'patrol patrol-points)
      (entity/set! eid 'patrol-index 0)
      (entity/set! eid 'behavior 'patrol)
      (entity/max-speed! eid 3.0)
      (entity/gravity-scale! eid 0)
      (ai/max-force! eid 0.8)
      eid)))

(define (animate-dragonfly eid frame)
  (let ((behavior (entity/get eid 'behavior)))
    (cond
      ((eq? behavior 'patrol)
       (let* ((patrol (entity/get eid 'patrol))
              (index (entity/get eid 'patrol-index))
              (waypoint (list-ref patrol index))
              (wx (car waypoint))
              (wy (cadr waypoint))
              (dist (entity/distance eid wx wy)))
         (ai/seek eid wx wy)
         (when (< dist 2.0)
           (entity/set! eid 'patrol-index (modulo (+ index 1) (length patrol))))))
      ((eq? behavior 'chase)
       (let ((target (entity/get eid 'target)))
         (when target
           (ai/pursue eid target)))))))

(big-bang
  (on-tick (lambda (state frame)
             (world/step)
             (world/each (lambda (e)
                           (when (eq? (entity/kind e) 'dragonfly)
                             (animate-dragonfly e frame))))
             state))
  (to-draw (lambda (state) #t)))
```

```motoi
      (entity/gravity-scale! eid 0)
      (ai/max-force! eid 0.8)
      eid)))

(define (animate-dragonfly eid frame)
  (let ((behavior (entity/get eid 'behavior)))
    (cond
      ((eq? behavior 'patrol)
       (let* ((patrol (entity/get eid 'patrol))
              (index (entity/get eid 'patrol-index))
              (waypoint (list-ref patrol index))
              (wx (car waypoint))
              (wy (cadr waypoint))
              (dist (entity/distance eid wx wy)))
         (ai/seek eid wx wy)
         (when (< dist 2.0)
           (entity/set! eid 'patrol-index (modulo (+ index 1) (length patrol))))))
      ((eq? behavior 'chase)
       (let ((target (entity/get eid 'target)))
         (when target
           (ai/pursue eid target)))))))

(big-bang
  (on-tick (lambda (state frame)
             (world/step)
             (world/each (lambda (e)
                           (when (eq? (entity/kind e) 'dragonfly)
                             (animate-dragonfly e frame))))
             state))
  (to-draw (lambda (state) #t)))
```
A dragonfly that patrols (chapter 12 waypoints) and, when it senses prey, pursues (chapter 10).

In Motoi Scheme, show me an example of the bee's brain.

```motoi
(define (bee-brain eid frame)
  (let ((nectar (entity/get eid 'nectar))
        (behavior (entity/get eid 'behavior))
        (target (entity/get eid 'target))
        (feed-timer (entity/get-set! eid 'feed-timer 0)))
    
    (cond
      ;; If feeding, decrement timer
      ((> feed-timer 0)
       (entity/set! eid 'feed-timer (- feed-timer 1))
       (when (<= (entity/get eid 'feed-timer) 0)
         (entity/set! eid 'behavior 'wander)
         (entity/set! eid 'target #f)))
      
      ;; If wandering, look for flowers
      ((eq? behavior 'wander)
       (let ((nearest (world/nearest eid 'flower 8.0)))
         (if nearest
             (begin
               (entity/set! eid 'behavior 'seek)
               (entity/set! eid 'target nearest))
             (ai/wander eid 1.0 0.3))))
      
      ;; If seeking, check arrival
      ((eq? behavior 'seek)
       (if (and target (entity/alive? target))
           (let ((dist (entity/distance eid (entity/x target) (entity/y target))))
             (if (< dist 1.5)
                 ;; Arrived: start feeding
                 (begin
                   (entity/set! eid 'behavior 'feed)
                   (entity/set! eid 'feed-timer 180)
                   (entity/set! eid 'nectar (+ nectar 50)))
                 ;; Still traveling
                 (ai/seek eid (entity/x target) (entity/y target))))
           ;; Target lost: resume wander
           (begin
             (entity/set! eid 'behavior 'wander)
             (entity/set! eid 'target #f)))))))

(big-bang

             (world/each (lambda (e)

                              (animate-flower e frame))
                             ((eq? (entity/kind e) 'bee)
                              (bee-brain e frame))
                             ((eq? (entity/kind e) 'dragonfly)
                              (animate-dragonfly e frame)))))
             state))
  (to-draw (lambda (state) #t)))
```

```motoi
(define (dragonfly-brain eid frame)
  (let ((behavior (entity/get eid 'behavior))
        (target (entity/get eid 'target))
        (hunger (entity/get-set! eid 'hunger 0)))
    
    ;; Hunger grows over time
    (entity/set! eid 'hunger (+ hunger 1))
    
    (cond
      ;; If patrolling and hungry, look for prey
      ((eq? behavior 'patrol)
       (animate-dragonfly eid frame)
       (when (> hunger 300)
         (let ((prey (world/nearest eid 'bee 12.0)))
           (when prey
             (entity/set! eid 'behavior 'chase)
             (entity/set! eid 'target prey)))))
      
      ;; If chasing, pursue and check capture
      ((eq? behavior 'chase)
       (if (and target (entity/alive? target))
           (let ((dist (entity/distance eid (entity/x target) (entity/y target))))
             (ai/pursue eid target)
             (when (< dist 1.0)
               ;; Captured: eat and return to patrol
               (entity/despawn! target)
               (entity/set! eid 'hunger 0)
               (entity/set! eid 'behavior 'patrol)
               (entity/set! eid 'target #f)))
           ;; Prey lost: return to patrol
           (begin
             (entity/set! eid 'behavior 'patrol)
             (entity/set! eid 'target #f)))))))
```

```motoi
(define (sky-color phase)
  (cond
    ((< phase 360) '(0.3 0.4 0.6))   ; dawn
    ((< phase 720) '(0.5 0.7 1.0))   ; day
    ((< phase 1080) '(0.8 0.5 0.3))  ; dusk

            (row (+ y 1))))
```

In Motoi Scheme, show me an example of particle effects: pollen.

```motoi
(define (emit-pollen x y)
  (let loop ((i 0))
    (when (< i 5)
      (let ((px (+ x (* (- (random) 0.5) 0.5)))
            (py (+ y (* (- (random) 0.5) 0.5)))
            (vx (* (- (random) 0.5) 0.2))
            (vy (* (- (random) 0.5) 0.2))
            (p (world/spawn px py 'pollen)))
        (entity/set-vel! p vx vy)
        (entity/gravity-scale! p -0.1)
        (entity/max-speed! p 0.5)
        (entity/drag! p 0.95)
        (entity/set! p 'lifetime 120)
        (loop (+ i 1))))))

(define (animate-pollen eid frame)
  (let* ((life (entity/get eid 'lifetime))
         (wind-x (car (world/wind)))
         (wind-y (cadr (world/wind))))
    (entity/accel! eid wind-x wind-y)
    (entity/set! eid 'lifetime (- life 1))
    (when (<= (entity/get eid 'lifetime) 0)
      (entity/despawn! eid))))

;; Modified bee-brain: emit pollen when feeding starts
(define (bee-brain-with-pollen eid frame)
  (let ((nectar (entity/get eid 'nectar))
        (behavior (entity/get eid 'behavior))
        (target (entity/get eid 'target))
        (feed-timer (entity/get-set! eid 'feed-timer 0)))
    
    (cond
      ((> feed-timer 0)
       (entity/set! eid 'feed-timer (- feed-timer 1))
       (when (= (modulo feed-timer 30) 0)
         (emit-pollen (entity/x eid) (entity/y eid)))
       (when (<= (entity/get eid 'feed-timer) 0)
         (entity/set! eid 'behavior 'wander)
         (entity/set! eid 'target #f)))
      
      ((eq? behavior 'wander)
       (let ((nearest (world/nearest eid 'flower 8.0)))
         (if nearest
             (begin
               (entity/set! eid 'behavior 'seek)
               (entity/set! eid 'target nearest))
             (ai/wander eid 1.0 0.3))))
      
      ((eq? behavior 'seek)
       (if (and target (entity/alive? target))
           (let ((dist (entity/distance eid (entity/x target) (entity/y target))))
             (if (< dist 1.5)
                 (begin
                   (entity/set! eid 'behavior 'feed)
                   (entity/set! eid 'feed-timer 180)
                   (entity/set! eid 'nectar (+ nectar 50))
                   (emit-pollen (entity/x eid) (entity/y eid)))
                 (ai/seek eid (entity/x target) (entity/y target))))
           (begin
             (entity/set! eid 'behavior 'wander)
             (entity/set! eid 'target #f)))))))
```

```motoi
(define (director-policy frame)
  (let ((bee-count (group/count 'bee))
        (dragonfly-count (group/count 'dragonfly))
        (hunger (if (> dragonfly-count 0)
                    (entity/get (world/find 'dragonfly) 'hunger)
                    0)))
    
    (cond
      ;; Bees thriving: add pressure
      ((> bee-count 8)
       (when (and (= dragonfly-count 1) (= (modulo frame 600) 0))
         ;; Spawn second dragonfly every 10 seconds
         (prefab/spawn 'dragonfly 35 5 '((30 5) (10 5) (10 15) (30 15))))
       (world/wind! (* 0.08 (dsin (* frame 0.005))) 0))
      
      ;; Bees struggling: ease pressure
      ((< bee-count 3)
       (when (> dragonfly-count 1)
         ;; Remove extra dragonfly
         (let ((df (world/find 'dragonfly)))
           (when (> (entity/get-set! df 'is-extra 0) 0)
             (entity/despawn! df))))
       (world/wind! (* 0.02 (dsin (* frame 0.005))) 0))
      
      ;; Balanced: maintain
      (else
       (world/wind! (* 0.05 (dsin (* frame 0.005))) 0)))))
```

The director (chapter 15) adjusts environmental pressure—wind strength, dragonfly spawn rate, flower growth—based on population health. If bees are thriving, spawn a second dragonfly.
In Motoi Scheme, show me an example of the living garden: complete.

```motoi
;; === PREFABS ===

(prefab/define 'flower
  (lambda (x y hue phase)
    (let ((eid (world/spawn x y 'flower)))
      (entity/sprite! eid 'blossom-5)
      (entity/set! eid 'hue hue)
      (entity/set! eid 'phase phase)
      (entity/gravity-scale! eid 0)
      (entity/max-speed! eid 0.5)
      (entity/drag! eid 0.9)
      eid)))

(prefab/define 'bee
  (lambda (x y)
    (let ((eid (world/spawn x y 'bee)))
      (entity/sprite! eid 'blossom-1)
      (entity/set! eid 'nectar 100)
      (entity/set! eid 'behavior 'wander)
      (entity/max-speed! eid 2.0)
      (entity/gravity-scale! eid 0)
      (ai/max-force! eid 0.5)
      eid)))

(prefab/define 'dragonfly
  (lambda (x y patrol-points)
    (let ((eid (world/spawn x y 'dragonfly)))
      (entity/sprite! eid 'blossom-9)
      (entity/set! eid 'patrol patrol-points)
      (entity/set! eid 'patrol-index 0)
      (entity/set! eid 'behavior 'patrol)
      (entity/set! eid 'hunger 0)
      (entity/max-speed! eid 3.0)
      (entity/gravity-scale! eid 0)
      (ai/max-force! eid 0.8)
      eid)))

;; === BRAINS ===

(define (animate-flower eid frame)
  (let* ((phase (entity/get eid 'phase))
         (sway (* 0.3 (dsin (+ (* frame 0.02) phase))))
         (wind-x (car (world/wind)))
         (wind-y (cadr (world/wind))))
    (part/sway eid 'stem sway)
    (entity/accel! eid wind-x wind-y)))

(define (bee-brain eid frame)
  (let ((nectar (entity/get eid 'nectar))
        (behavior (entity/get eid 'behavior))
        (target (entity/get eid 'target))
        (feed-timer (entity/get-set! eid 'feed-timer 0)))
    (cond
      ((> feed-timer 0)
       (entity/set! eid 'feed-timer (- feed-timer 1))
       (when (= (modulo feed-timer 30) 0)
         (emit-pollen (entity/x eid) (entity/y eid)))
       (when (<= (entity/get eid 'feed-timer) 0)
         (entity/set! eid 'behavior 'wander)
         (entity/set! eid 'target #f)))
      ((eq? behavior 'wander)
       (let ((nearest (world/nearest eid 'flower 8.0)))
         (if nearest
             (begin
               (entity/set! eid 'behavior 'seek)
               (entity/set! eid 'target nearest))
             (ai/wander eid 1.0 0.3))))
      ((eq? behavior 'seek)
       (if (and target (entity/alive? target))
           (let ((dist (entity/distance eid (entity/x target) (entity/y target))))
             (if (< dist 1.5)
                 (begin
                   (entity/set! eid 'behavior 'feed)
                   (entity/set! eid 'feed-timer 180)
                   (entity/set! eid 'nectar (+ nectar 50))
                   (emit-pollen (entity/x eid) (entity/y eid)))
                 (ai/seek eid (entity/x target) (entity/y target))))
           (begin
             (entity/set! eid 'behavior 'wander)
             (entity/set! eid 'target #f)))))))

(define (dragonfly-brain eid frame)
  (let ((behavior (entity/get eid 'behavior))
        (target (entity/get eid 'target))
        (hunger (entity/get-set! eid 'hunger 0)))
    (entity/set! eid 'hunger (+ hunger 1))
    (cond
      ((eq? behavior 'patrol)
       (let* ((patrol (entity/get eid 'patrol))
              (index (entity/get eid 'patrol-index))
              (waypoint (list-ref patrol index))
              (wx (car waypoint))
              (wy (cadr waypoint))
              (dist (entity/distance eid wx wy)))
         (ai/seek eid wx wy)
         (when (< dist 2.0)
           (entity/set! eid 'patrol-index (modulo (+ index 1) (length patrol)))))
       (when (> hunger 300)
         (let ((prey (world/nearest eid 'bee 12.0)))
           (when prey
             (entity/set! eid 'behavior 'chase)
             (entity/set! eid 'target prey)))))
      ((eq? behavior 'chase)
       (if (and target (entity/alive? target))
           (let ((dist (entity/distance eid (entity/x target) (entity/y target))))
             (ai/pursue eid target)
             (when (< dist 1.0)
               (entity/despawn! target)
               (entity/set! eid 'hunger 0)
               (entity/set! eid 'behavior 'patrol)
               (entity/set! eid 'target #f)))
           (begin
             (entity/set! eid 'behavior 'patrol)
             (entity/set! eid 'target #f)))))))

(define (emit-pollen x y)
  (let loop ((i 0))
    (when (< i 5)
      (let ((px (+ x (* (- (random) 0.5) 0.5)))
            (py (+ y (* (- (random) 0.5) 0.5)))
            (vx (* (- (random) 0.5) 0.2))
            (vy (* (- (random) 0.5) 0.2))
            (p (world/spawn px py 'pollen)))
        (entity/set-vel! p vx vy)
        (entity/gravity-scale! p -0.1)
        (entity/max-speed! p 0.5)
        (entity/drag! p 0.95)
        (entity/set! p 'lifetime 120)
        (loop (+ i 1))))))

(define (animate-pollen eid frame)
  (let* ((life (entity/get eid 'lifetime))
         (wind-x (car (world/wind)))
         (wind-y (cadr (world/wind))))
    (entity/accel! eid wind-x wind-y)
    (entity/set! eid 'lifetime (- life 1))
    (when (<= (entity/get eid 'lifetime) 0)
      (entity/despawn! eid))))

;; === ENVIRONMENT ===

(define (day-phase frame)
  (modulo frame 1440))

(define (sky-color phase)
  (cond
    ((< phase 360) '(0.3 0.4 0.6))
    ((< phase 720) '(0.5 0.7 1.0))
    ((< phase 1080) '(0.8 0.5 0.3))
    (else '(0.1 0.1 0.2))))

(define (draw-sky frame)
  (let ((c (sky-color (day-phase frame))))
    (let loop ((x 0))
      (when (< x 40)
        (let row ((y 0))
          (when (< y 2)
            (paint-cell x y c)
            (row (+ y 1))))
        (loop (+ x 1))))))

(define (draw-ground)
  (let loop ((x 0))
    (when (< x 40)
      (let row ((y 2))
        (when (< y 30)
          (let ((c (cond
                     ((< y 20) '(0.4 0.6 0.3))
                     (else '(0.5 0.4 0.3)))))
            (paint-cell x y c))
          (row (+ y 1))))
      (loop (+ x 1)))))

;; === DIRECTOR ===

(define (director-policy frame)
  (let ((bee-count (group/count 'bee))
        (dragonfly-count (group/count 'dragonfly)))
    (cond
      ((> bee-count 8)
       (when (and (= dragonfly-count 1) (= (modulo frame 600) 0))
         (let ((df (prefab/spawn 'dragonfly 35 5 '((30 5) (10 5) (10 15) (30 15)))))
           (entity/set! df 'is-extra 1)))
       (world/wind! (* 0.08 (dsin (* frame 0.005))) 0))
      ((< bee-count 3)
       (when (> dragonfly-count 1)
         (world/each (lambda (e)
                       (when (and (eq? (entity/kind e) 'dragonfly)
                                  (> (entity/get-set! e 'is-extra 0) 0))
                         (entity/despawn! e)))))
       (world/wind! (* 0.02 (dsin (* frame 0.005))) 0))
      (else
       (world/wind! (* 0.05 (dsin (* frame 0.005))) 0)))))

;; === CAMERA ===

(define (pick-camera-subject)
  (let ((dragonfly (world/find 'dragonfly)))
    (if (and dragonfly (eq? (entity/get dragonfly 'behavior) 'chase))
        (let ((target (entity/get dragonfly 'target)))
          (if target target dragonfly))
        (let ((feeding-bee #f))
          (world/each (lambda (e)
                        (when (and (not feeding-bee)
                                   (eq? (entity/kind e) 'bee)
                                   (eq? (entity/get e 'behavior) 'feed))
                          (set! feeding-bee e))))
          (if feeding-bee feeding-bee dragonfly)))))

(define (update-camera frame)
  (let ((subject (pick-camera-subject)))
    (when subject
      (world/camera-follow! subject 0.05))))

;; === SPAWN ===

(define (spawn-garden)
  (world/reset!)
  (draw-ground)
  (let loop ((i 0))
    (when (< i 20)
      (let ((x (+ 5 (* i 1.5)))
            (y (+ 10 (* (dsin (* i 0.5)) 2)))
            (hue (* i 0.05))
            (phase (* i 0.3)))
        (prefab/spawn 'flower x y hue phase))
      (loop (+ i 1))))
  (let loop ((i 0))
    (when (< i 5)
      (prefab/spawn 'bee (+ 10 (* i 5)) 5)
      (loop (+ i 1))))
  (prefab/spawn 'dragonfly 20 5 '((10 5) (30 5) (30 15) (10 15)))
  (world/wind! 0.02 0))

;; === RUN ===

(spawn-garden)

(big-bang
  (on-tick (lambda (state frame)
             (draw-sky frame)
             (director-policy frame)
             (world/step)
             (update-camera frame)
             (world/each (lambda (e)
                           (cond
                             ((eq? (entity/kind e) 'flower)
                              (animate-flower e frame))
                             ((eq? (entity/kind e) 'bee)
                              (bee-brain e frame))
                             ((eq? (entity/kind e) 'dragonfly)
                              (dragonfly-brain e frame))
                             ((eq? (entity/kind e) 'pollen)
                              (animate-pollen e frame)))))
             state))
  (to-draw (lambda (state) #t)))
```
